meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Like a Pro: Student Guide to Mastering Client Workflows
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with code bugs. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster and build better portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: To solve debugging gridlock, adopt a 'Client-First' workflow. By treating your codebase like a professional client project, you isolate logic, document state transitions, and leverage AI agents like Claude Code to perform automated root-cause analysis. This systematic approach transforms chaotic troubleshooting into a repeatable, high-velocity engineering process.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, your console is flooded with red errors, and the logic that worked ten minutes ago has mysteriously evaporated. It is the rite of passage for every student developer: the moment you realize that writing code is easy, but debugging it is where you actually become an engineer. The frustration isn't a sign of failure; it's a sign that your current workflow lacks the structure needed for scale.

We are going to replace that aimless guessing with a professional-grade client workflow. By integrating Antigravity’s precision analysis and Claude Code’s iterative reasoning, you will stop fighting your code and start commanding it. This isn't just about fixing a bug—it’s about building the architectural habits that separate top-tier developers from the rest of the pack.

steps.title: Establish the 'Client' Scope
steps.description: Before fixing anything, define the bug as a feature request. This mental shift prevents 'code drift' where you break more than you fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase and identify the specific module causing the error. Act as a senior consultant: define the 'client expectation' for this module vs current output.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/core --focus 'Identify the logic flow of the failing module'

steps.title: Isolate the State Transition
steps.description: Bugs usually hide in the gaps between functions. Map out how data flows through your app to find where it mutates incorrectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the state variables in this file. Create a visual map of how data enters and exits the function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Trace the state mutation flow in the current component and highlight where the variable becomes undefined.'

steps.title: Automate the Reproduction
steps.description: You cannot fix what you cannot consistently break. Create a minimal test case that triggers the bug every time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a minimal reproduction script for this bug. Ensure it is isolated from the rest of the application logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a temporary test file that triggers the identified error 100% of the time'

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Use Claude Code to propose a fix, but treat the AI as a junior dev. Review the logic before applying the patch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix for potential side effects. Does this maintain the architectural integrity of the project?
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'Apply the proposed patch to resolve the reproduction script error without introducing side effects'

steps.title: Validation and Clean-up
steps.description: Once the bug is dead, ensure the 'Client' (the codebase) is left in a better state than you found it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the final code for readability and performance. Suggest one optimization to prevent this bug from recurring.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review --all --comment 'Verify the fix and suggest defensive coding patterns to prevent regression'

pro_tips: Always commit your 'broken' state to a separate branch before debugging; it’s your safety net for 'what if' scenarios.
Use Claude Code to explain your code to you like you are a five-year-old; if you can't explain it, you don't understand the bug.
Treat console.log() as a last resort; learn to use the debugger tool in your IDE to inspect the call stack in real-time.
Limit your debugging sessions to 45 minutes; if you haven't found the root cause, step away for 10 minutes to reset your cognitive load.
Document your fix in a README.md 'Troubleshooting' section; your future self and your portfolio readers will thank you.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your project using this workflow, push your code to GitHub and write a brief 'Post-Mortem' in your commit history explaining the bug and your fix. This is the single most impressive thing you can show a future employer.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs stall your progress and start building with professional-grade momentum. Every bug you squash is a lesson in architecture that makes you a stronger, more hireable developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
